pattern: Splitting the digit run into two halves and, within each half, enumerating every legal decimal-point placement that avoids leading and trailing zeroes generates all original coordinate pairs.
hints: The comma can fall after any interior position, splitting the digits into a left number and a right number that you format independently.
For one digit run, list every way to insert a decimal point (or none) that yields a valid number with no extraneous zeroes, then pair the left options with the right options.

A run is a valid integer only if it has no leading zero (unless it is exactly "0"); it is a valid decimal a.b only if the integer part a has no leading zero and the fractional part b has no trailing zero.

edge_inputs.description: first statement example, several splits and decimal placements
leading-zero run forces single-zero or 0.x forms only
many zeros sharply restrict legal placements
two digits, the smallest interesting case
trailing zero blocks some decimal fractions
all zeros, only the single integer zero is legal on each side
ten digit run with mixed zero placement

stress.description: random digit strings within the length bound wrapped in parentheses
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 10)
    digits = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    return ['(' + digits + ')']
stress.note: small inputs because the enumeration is combinatorial
